L1.area: Report template compilation and script execution engine

L2.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where user-supplied expressions in report templates are compiled and executed as .NET code on the server without any restrictions on the types, methods, or assemblies that can be referenced, allowing arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: Report script compilation pipeline and compiler security configuration
L3.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where user-supplied expressions in report templates are compiled into .NET assemblies and executed on the server without any validation or restriction of the referenced types and methods. An attacker can leverage the web-based report designer to inject arbitrary .NET code through report expressions, gaining full access to the server's file system, processes, and network.

ground_truth.reason: FastReport compiles and executes user-supplied C# expressions from report templates as .NET assemblies without any restrictions on accessible types, methods, or assemblies, allowing arbitrary code execution on the server.
